about.historyText: With 35 years of professional experience as a session drummer, I have had the privilege of recording with international artists including John Legend, Alejandro Sanz, and Juanes. My professional remote recording service delivers world-class drum tracks from my acoustically designed studio in Barcelona.

faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subheading: Find answers to the most common questions about the recording process
faq.noAnswerDesc: If you have any specific questions about your project or the recording process, don't hesitate to contact me directly. I'll be happy to help.
faq.q1: What does the online drum recording service consist of?
faq.a1: It consists of professionally recording acoustic drums for your songs. We can record drums for your tracks. Using modern technology, we can do this from our studio with maximum quality and deliver the recording online anywhere in the world, saving up to 70% of costs while achieving professional sound.
faq.q2: What studio equipment do you use?
faq.a2: The best. Microphones, drums, snares, etc. You can see it in our 'Studio' section.
faq.a3: Toni Mateos. With over 35 years of experience and more than 1000 recorded albums, including John Legend, Alejandro Sanz, Jarabe de Palo, Raphael, Rozalén, Antonio Orozco, Pastora Soler...
faq.a4: Most styles: pop, rock, funk, jazz, blues, swing, R&B, hip hop, rap, folk, country, ballad, salsa, samba, bossa nova, Latin rhythms, etc. We currently do not record metal.
faq.q5: Do you offer a satisfaction guarantee?
faq.a5: Absolutely. If you're not satisfied, we'll refund 100% of your money.
faq.a6: You decide, by configuring the recording session that best suits your needs.
faq.q7: My country's currency is not the euro. Is that a problem?
faq.a7: No. We accept payments from any country in the world.
faq.a8: Via PayPal, bank transfer, or credit card.
faq.q9: Can we discuss the details before recording?
faq.a9: Of course. We'd love to connect with you via WhatsApp or email so you can tell us in detail what kind of sound you're looking for. If you need a video call, you can request one in our price configurator.
faq.q10: How will I receive the recording?
faq.a10: After recording for you, you'll receive an email to download all the tracks.
faq.a11: No, we record multi-tracks, ready for you to mix yourself.
faq.a12: Yes, quantised with Beat Detective so they're perfectly in time and error-free. Ready to mix.
faq.q13: What if I don't like something about the recording?
faq.q14: Can I request a free sample of your tracks?
faq.a14: Yes, you can download sample tracks in the 'Download a Sample' section.
faq.q15: How long will it take to receive the tracks?
faq.a15: You can decide in our price configurator.
faq.q16: Are the tracks processed with any EQ, compression or effects?
faq.q17: What format are the drum tracks in? Are they compatible with my DAW?
faq.a17: We send WAV files, fully compatible with any recording and mixing software (Pro Tools, Logic, Cubase, Reaper...).
faq.q18: What will be the bit/sample rate of the tracks?
faq.q19: How will I know which mics and preamps were used in my recording?
faq.a19: You'll receive a technical sheet detailing the microphones, preamps and converters used for each channel.
faq.q20: I have a song with drums already arranged (MIDI, loops...). Can you copy it exactly?
faq.a20: Yes, no problem. You'll need to select this option in our price configurator. You also have the option of us recording a very similar but not identical drum part (included in the basic plan), or recording an additional take with fresh new ideas.
faq.q21: I have a nearly finished song but haven't programmed the drums. Is that a problem?
faq.a21: No, we can compose the drums for your song. Contact us and we'll work out the details.
faq.q22: Does my song need to be recorded with a click/metronome?
faq.q23: What do I need to send for you to start recording my song?
faq.a23: Just a few things: a stereo WAV audio file of your song WITHOUT DRUMS; a stereo WAV audio file of your song with DRUMS ONLY (MIDI programming, loops, etc.); the bit/sample rate of your project; the exact tempo in BPMs (if the tempo varies, we'll need the tempo map .mid file).
faq.q24: Does my production need to be finished before you record the drums?

blog.heading: Blog
blog.subheading: Articles about drum recording techniques, studio gear and Toni Mateos' career as a professional online session drummer

post1.title: How to Record Drums Online Professionally
post1.description: Discover the complete process to get professional drum tracks recorded remotely — from setup configuration to final delivery by an experienced session drummer.
post1.content:
Online drum recording has revolutionised music production. You no longer need to book a local studio or coordinate schedules with a session drummer in your city. From Groove Factory Studios in Barcelona, Toni Mateos offers a professional remote drum recording service combining 35 years of experience with world-class gear.
The process is simple: configure your session by choosing microphones, preamps and audio interfaces, send your demo with style notes, and receive multitrack stems in high quality (24-bit/48kHz) straight to your inbox.
The key to a great result is communication. The more details you provide about the sound you're after — Spotify references, groove indications, exact BPMs — the closer the final tracks will be to your vision. Toni has recorded over 1,000 albums with artists including John Legend, Alejandro Sanz, Juanes, Antonio Orozco and Pastora Soler, and can adapt to any style.
The 50 m² room with 3.5-metre ceilings is designed to capture a natural, powerful drum sound. The audio chain features Neumann, AKG and Sennheiser microphones, API, Neve and DAD preamps, and state-of-the-art converters.
If you're looking for professional drum tracks without leaving your studio, online session drumming with Toni Mateos is the answer.

post2.title: Best Microphones for Recording Drums
post2.description: A complete guide to the microphones we use at Groove Factory Studios to capture the best drum sound: Neumann, AKG, Sennheiser and more.
post2.content:
Microphone choice is one of the most decisive factors in the quality of a drum recording. At Groove Factory Studios we use a carefully curated arsenal to cover every element of the kit.
For the snare, the industry standard is the Shure SM57, but when we want more body and detail we add an AKG C414 underneath or a Sennheiser MD441 to capture every nuance of the snare wires.
On the kick drum, the combination of a Beta 52 with a Beta 91A inside provides attack and definition without losing low end. For a more vintage character, the Neumann U47 FET is unbeatable.
Overheads are critical for the stereo image. We use a pair of AKG C414s in XY or ORTF configuration, and for a softer texture the Coles 4038 ribbon mics deliver that unmistakable British warmth.
Room microphones — a Neumann U87 and an AKG C12 — bring the 50 m² room to life, capturing the natural reflections that make the drums sound three-dimensional.
Each microphone can be added or removed from the session configurator, letting you tailor the exact setup to your production's needs.

post3.title: API, Neve and DAD Preamps: What's the Difference?
post3.description: A technical comparison of the three legendary preamp families we use in the studio: API for punch, Neve for vintage warmth, DAD for absolute transparency.
post3.content:
Preamplifiers are the hidden soul of any recording. While microphones capture the sound, preamps give it character and colour. At Groove Factory Studios we have three legendary families: API, Neve and DAD.
API means punch. Its transformers deliver a presence and power that work especially well on rock and pop drums. It's the sound of the great American records: direct, muscular and full-bodied.
Neve means warmth. Its circuitry adds a smoothness and musicality that rounds off transients without losing definition. It's the go-to preamp for productions seeking a vintage, organic touch. Run a snare through a Neve and the difference is instant.
DAD (Digital Audio Denmark) represents total transparency. Its preamps add no colouration: what goes in is exactly what comes out, with exceptional dynamic range. It's the choice for productions demanding maximum fidelity — classical music, jazz or film scores.
In our session configurator you can choose between the basic MOTU kit (included) or upgrade to the legendary API + Neve + DAD pack. Most of our professional clients choose the upgrade because the difference in the final result is dramatic.

post4.title: Toni Mateos: 35 Years Setting the Rhythm in Music
post4.description: The professional career of Toni Mateos, online session drummer with 1,000+ albums recorded and collaborations with John Legend, Alejandro Sanz, Juanes and more.
post4.content:
With over 35 years as a professional musician, Toni Mateos is one of the most sought-after session drummers in the Spanish-language music scene — and increasingly on the international stage. His CV includes over 1,000 recorded albums and more than 2,000 live artists.
His list of collaborations features names such as John Legend, Alejandro Sanz, Juanes, Antonio Orozco, Ana Torroja, Franco de Vita, Pastora Soler, Manuel Carrasco, Sergio Dalma, Raphael, Rosario Flores and La Guardia, among many others.
Beyond live performance, Toni has appeared on over 500 television shows and records more than 800 songs a year at his Barcelona studio. His versatility allows him to move naturally between pop, rock, flamenco, Latin ballad and any style that demands groove and musicality.
Groove Factory Studios was born as the natural extension of this experience. A purpose-built 50 m² space designed specifically for drum recording, equipped with the finest microphones and preamps on the market.
Today, thanks to his online remote drum recording service, musicians and producers worldwide can access Toni Mateos' sound and expertise without leaving their own studio. From Barcelona to the world.

post5.title: How to Prepare Your DAW Project for a Professional Remote Drum Session
post5.description: A step-by-step guide to preparing your audio project to ensure a flawless professional remote drum session with Groove Factory Studios.
post5.content:
The transition from a home demo to a professional production often begins with the quality of the rhythm section. For an online session drummer to deliver the best results, the initial project preparation is a critical step that ensures technical compatibility and musical alignment. When working with Groove Factory Studios, the first requirement is a clear, consolidated audio file of your song. This track should ideally be a high-resolution WAV file, exported from the very first beat of the project to ensure perfect synchronization. Providing a separate click track or at least specifying the exact BPM and time signature is essential for maintaining the rhythmic integrity that a professional recording demands.
Technical specifications also play a significant role in the outcome of a remote session. It is recommended to export your guide tracks with enough headroom to avoid clipping, allowing the drummer to hear the nuances of the arrangement. If your song features specific rhythmic hooks or sections where the drums should follow a particular melody, including a MIDI file of your programmed drums can serve as an excellent reference point. This allows the session drummer to understand your original vision while applying the organic feel and professional touch that only 30 years of experience can provide. Once these files are ready, the communication regarding the desired sound and references will ensure that the final drum stems integrate seamlessly into your mix.

post6.title: Real Drums vs Programmed Beats: Why Organic Rhythm Still Wins
post6.description: A comparative analysis of programmed drums versus professional live session recording: dynamics, character and sonic advantages.
post6.content:
In the era of digital precision, the debate between using programmed drum libraries and hiring a professional session drummer remains more relevant than ever. While software provides an immediate and cost-effective solution for demoing, it often lacks the micro-timing and dynamic variations that define a world-class record. An organic performance brings a human element that responds to the emotional shifts of a song, something that static samples cannot replicate. With a career spanning over 1000 recorded albums, the expertise involved in choosing the right snare tension or cymbal wash for a specific verse is what separates a good track from a hit record.
The technical advantage of recording at a specialised facility like Groove Factory Studios involves more than just a human performance; it includes the use of high-end analogue signal chains. Capturing a real kit through legendary preamps like Neve or API creates a harmonic richness that digital emulations often miss. This depth and air around the kit provide a spatial dimension to the mix that helps the vocals and instruments sit naturally. When a producer chooses a live session, they are not just buying a rhythm; they are investing in the legacy and sonic character of an artist who has toured with global stars like Alejandro Sanz and Ricky Martin, bringing that same stadium-level energy to an independent production.

post7.title: 30 Years of Session Drumming: Lessons from Global Stars
post7.description: Reflections on three decades of professional drumming, from touring with Juanes and Miguel Bosé to remote recording from Barcelona for the world.
post7.content:
Success in the music industry is rarely built overnight, and for a professional drummer, it is often the result of thousands of hours in the studio and on the road. Reflecting on a 30-year career, the lessons learned from working with artists like Juanes, Miguel Bosé, and Alejandro Sanz have shaped a unique approach to session work. One of the most vital insights gained from these high-pressure environments is the importance of listening over playing. In a professional session, the goal is never to showcase technical virtuosity for its own sake, but to serve the song and support the artist's vision. This discipline is what allowed for participation in over 500 television shows and countless international tours.
Beyond the musicality, the professional standard expected at this level involves a deep understanding of studio technology and communication. Recording remotely from Barcelona for the world requires a level of trust that is only earned through consistent quality and reliability. Every session at Groove Factory Studios is approached with the same rigour as a major label project, ensuring that every drum stem delivered meets the highest industry standards. Whether it is a pop ballad or an energetic rock track, the experience of having shared the stage with figures like Alejandro Fernández or Antonio Orozco provides a perspective on arrangement and groove that significantly enhances the final production.

post8.title: Drumming for Alejandro Sanz: Recording «Y, ¿si fuera ella?»
post8.description: The story behind recording «Y, ¿si fuera ella?» for Alejandro Sanz's +ES+ album — one of the most emotional moments of my career.
post8.content:
One of the things I am most passionate about in my career as a musician is recording drums for different artists. As Plato once said: «music is for the soul, what gymnastics is to the body». Without a doubt, music is an art that endures over time, so leaving my mark on it is more than just magical for me. Working as a session drummer is an exciting job full of surprises. You have the opportunity to collaborate on different projects, record albums, soundtracks, even television shows. However, there are experiences that mark you forever and songs that stay with you for a lifetime. Mine has a title: «Y, ¿si fuera ella?» by Alejandro Sanz.
Recording drums for an Alejandro Sanz track has been the closest thing to a dream for me. I have always admired his compositions, his artistic career, and his talent, but furthermore, my idols are some of the drummers who have recorded on his albums: Vinnie Colaiuta, Lee Levin, Horacio "el Negro", and Alfredo Golino. Without a doubt, recording for Alejandro Sanz has been one of the most emotional moments of my musical career.
This journey began during a cold 2017, just after finishing my work on the TV show La Voz and while performing concerts with Antonio Orozco. Between gigs, I was practising at my rehearsal space when I received a call from producer José Luis de la Peña asking if I could record on Tuesday the 17th. José Luis is always involved in interesting projects, so I simply asked for the location and time: Estudios Cata in Madrid at 10 am.
Shortly after, sound engineer Felipe Guevara called to discuss the technical details. When I asked which artist it was for, he was surprised I did not know. I was going to record a song for Alejandro Sanz's new album, +ES+, featuring collaborators like Shakira, Malú, Manuel Carrasco, and Bisbal. My excitement was at its peak upon learning I would be a drummer for Alejandro Sanz.
On the day of the session, I arrived early to avoid stress and was greeted by resident engineer Manuel Pájaro. After setting up the drums, the other musicians arrived: Alfonso Pérez, pianist for Alejandro Sanz, and Alain Pérez, bassist for Paco de Lucía. It was an incredible lineup.
The session was conducted entirely live, meaning we played all at once rather than in separate parts as is common in many modern sessions. Playing live encourages the appearance of magic, or what we call duende, as each musician is enriched by the performance of their colleagues. Producer José Luis de la Peña gave us a single instruction: we were going to record «Y, ¿si fuera ella?» but with a more modern and current feel.
While Felipe finished setting up the microphones, we discussed how to approach the song. It needed to sound simple but powerful. The original 1997 version was over five and a half minutes long and contained tempo changes, a resource rarely used today. We decided to shorten it and play to a steady metronome. My DW Performance kit, with its large 24-inch bass drum and powerful toms, met the sonic requirements perfectly. After a few changes to my Zildjian cymbal set, we were ready.
We recorded only three takes, and in my view, they were incredible. Alfonso Pérez is sensitivity personified, soaking the room in his feeling and musicality, while Alain Pérez has an exquisite taste and bass precision worthy of admiration. The most beautiful part of the session was that we had fun and felt truly moved. At the end of the third take, we shared a great hug. I still get goosebumps thinking about it.
We knew we had just recorded the definitive take, and although the studio was reserved for the rest of the day, we did not want to repeat a single note. Felipe Guevara, who later handled the mixing, confirmed days later that he did not have to retouch anything. The album was released in June 2017 as a live album containing this single studio track. There are certainly experiences that change your life, and this has been one of the most emotional of my career.
